author: @TeddyWalker-e6s
text: I know that it is as a result of not associating myself with trends and not being active on social media. I have heard the name Ama Burland before, but have not paid any attention to it, just as I do with others. I have also heard about getting a mentor for one's self, but have not heeded. However, this podcast has given me a different perspective. This is my second time watching. The first was the one with Nana Aba which made me follow Women of Valour and this one with Ama Burland is my second. I loved every bit of the conversation. I laughed it off when I watched the teaser and heard her mention GH 50.00 as her start up money. I was like I have heard that before and laughed it off. But watching to the end, I have a different mindset now and although I am not younger than her, I choose her to be my mentor because I am free spirited as she is and love how she views things and want to be as successful as she is. Today marks the beginning of me rewriting my story. Thanks very much Konnected Minds, Derrick, and Ama Burland. Girl, you do all and I love you already🥰

author: @gh_blaqsoap2846
text: The waiting till one can afford 3X the cost of something is an advice that should be directed to the state/the government (of GH). The average person (who eventually becomes politicians) should also have that awareness at the back of their mind. We tend to think the state (coffers) is an institution to exploit and make money from as opposed to the other way around. They embark on projects (most often with funds borrowed from the IMF) mainly for the procurement contract "benefit", without maintenance arrangements in place. The state has to be wealthier to be able to protect its citizens. I watched a video reel sometime back and listened to Kwame Nkrumah's speech during the inauguration ceremony of the Akosombo dam. That speech literally was the beginning of his end! He spoke from both sides of his mouth even as representatives of Kaiser Aluminum (an American company that had funded the dam) were in attendance. America got rid of him (thru the C IA) to "protect" an American company's investment.
